excerpt: The virtues of Ali are countless in their abundance
Whoever acknowledges one of Ali's virtues, Allah will forgive him his previous and subsequent sins
Whoever writes down any of Ali's virtues—the angels shall ever beseech forgiveness for him so long as that writing endures
Whoever listens to an account of Ali's virtue, Allah shall forgive him all the sins his ears have incurred
Whoever looks upon any of Ali's virtues, Allah shall forgive him all the sins his eyes have committed
Even if the forests became pens, the sea became ink, the jinn became accountants, and mankind became scribes, they could not enumerate the virtues of the Commander of the Faithful, Ali ibn Abi Talib
I am the City of Knowledge, and Ali is its Gate

Whoever wishes to see Adam in his knowledge, Noah in his piety, Abraham in his intimacy with Allah, Moses in his majesty, Jesus in his asceticism, Solomon in his wisdom, and Job in his patience—let him look upon the face of Ali ibn Abi Talib
When you comprehend Ali, the teaching is complete
It suffices as proof of Maulana Ali's virtue that doubt itself concerning him is unbelief
O people! The first of the believers is Ali ibn Abi Talib
source_work: Prophetic statement at gathering of companions
source_location_hint: Jaʿfar ibn Mansur al-Yaman's Kitab al-ʿAlim wa-l-Ghulam

excerpt: Ali is nearest to me among kinfolk, most learned in faith, and the one who fights the enemies of Allah with the sword. He is my brother, my cousin, and after me, my caliph... He is the Banner of Praise and the Divider of Fire and Paradise
source_location_hint: Kirmani's al-Riyad and al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama

excerpt: Verily, We have granted you al-Kawthar
Verily, al-Kawthar al-tahir is your successor Ali. Indeed, your enemy is Abu Bakr
He said: This path is Ali—straight and direct
My Lord is Ali, and the straight path belongs to Him
The path is Ali
The numerical value of 'There is no god but He' is 110—the greatest name, and that number is the name of Ali

The tyrant gnaws his hands, regretting that he did not follow the path with the Messenger. The verse alludes to the four caliphs—Abu Bakr, Umar ibn al-Khattab, and Uthman ibn Affan, as opposed to Ali ibn Abi Talib
Ali is the inner Qur'an whom the people of the Prophet abandoned and neglected
Al-Rahman refers to Ali, and al-Rahim refers to Muhammad
This verse signifies the steadfastness of the truthful in their testimony concerning Ali and their unfailing devotion
The Prophet is in the likeness of Mary's lineage, while Ali is in the likeness of Mary's son—both divinely protected and exalted
Among the titles of Maulana Ali are: the Eye of Allah, the Tongue of Allah, and the Hand of Allah
source_location_hint: Kirmani's theological works and Al-Muʾayyad's Majalis

excerpt: Ali and I are of one light
source_work: Prophetic hadith on the unity of Muhammad and Ali

excerpt: Maulana Ali was born within the Kaaba itself
No scholar possessed deeper knowledge of the Scripture than Ali, and he was the first to testify to the Messenger
Allah refers to Ali through 'Minka' in Qur'an 9:3, the numerical values of which are equal: P(70) + L(30) + E(10) = 110 (Ali); M(40) + N(50) + K(10) = 110 (Minka)
Whomever I am master of, Ali is his master—proclaimed at Ghadir Khumm
source_location_hint: Kirmani's al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama; Al-Muʾayyad's al-Majalis

excerpt: The ummah said to the Messenger: 'You have guided us. What recompense shall we give you?' He replied: 'By my Lord, you are my reward—Ali!'
O Messenger of Allah, the Lord of East and West is He whose numerical value equals 110—without peer or equal. Make him your trustee
Both names Muhammad and Ali are among the Divine Beautiful Names. These were not names borne in the Age of Ignorance. Hamd derives from Muhammad and Ahmad; the highest derives from Ali the Supreme

The letter mim in Muhammad equals 40; the letter ain in Ali equals 70, totaling 110—the numerical value of Ali. The remaining letters of Muhammad sum to 52; those of Ali sum to 40, which together equal 92—the numerical value of Muhammad
The sum of both numbers, 202, equals the numerical value of Rabb (Lord), indicating that Muhammad the Messenger of Allah and Ali the Friend of Allah together embody the meaning of Lord
When one sums the letters of Muhammad's name in the Emanators, the total equals the numerical value of Islam; likewise, the sum of Ali's letters in the Emanators equals the numerical value of Faith
Mahmud and Ayaz stood in a single row; there was neither master nor servant
I shall tell you why the letter ain opens wide: The disbeliever hopes I shall not swallow him in one bite!
source_work: Poetic composition attributed to Sayyidna Ghulam Husain Sahib

excerpt: Allah the Almighty has created all of His creation in pairs—both the tangible things visible on the outward plane and the intelligible realities imperceptible to the senses.
source_location_hint: doctrine of cosmic pairs and complementary principles

excerpt: The Messenger based his religion upon this same divine pattern, adapting the method of worship and guidance according to divine wisdom which is the supreme manner.
source_location_hint: alignment of religion with cosmic creation

excerpt: Allah says in His Book: He created the heavens and the earth in six days, and on the seventh day He established Himself upon the Throne.
The gate is the door through which Allah has commanded us to enter the house; the verse would be utterly nonsensical to issue such an obvious command without deep meaning.
source_location_hint: taʾwil of Quranic gates and doors

excerpt: The Prophet declared on the day of Ghadīr Khumm: Whosoever's master I am, ʿAlī is his master.
source_location_hint: Ghadīr tradition in early Ismaili sources

excerpt: When ʿAbd Allāh ibn ʿAbbās was asked who the relatives of the Prophet are, he replied: These are ʿAlī, Fāṭima, al-Ḥasan, and al-Ḥusayn.
source_location_hint: identification of Ahl al-Bayt in Ghadīr context

excerpt: You are the best community brought forth for mankind.
The Prophet said to ʿAlī: ʿAlī is the best of all human beings. Whoever denies this has committed disbelief.
The Prophet said: I shall carry them, for just as their rider is noble, so too are these two riders, and their father is better than both of them.
source_location_hint: virtue narrative of al-Ḥasan and al-Ḥusayn

excerpt: O people! Shall I not inform you of those whose father, mother, grandfather, grandmother, uncles, aunts, Shīʿa and lovers are among the people of Paradise?
source_location_hint: extended virtues narrative of Ahl al-Bayt

excerpt: Al-Ḥasan and al-Ḥusayn are the two leaders of the youth of Paradise, and their father is better than both of them.
source_location_hint: Imams as leaders of Paradise tradition

excerpt: Maulana al-Ḥākim then turned to the preacher and said: Now you may call the adhān as you wish. No one shall be permitted to object.
source_location_hint: narrative of al-Ḥākim al-Mustanṣir and the Abyssinian

excerpt: Allah testifies that there is no deity except Him.
Allah wishes to remove all uncleanness from the Ahl al-Bayt and to purify them with a perfect purification.
source_location_hint: Ayat al-Tathir and purity of Ahl al-Bayt

excerpt: Peace be upon the family of Yāsīn; thus do We reward the virtuous.
source_location_hint: identification of Ahl al-Bayt as Āl Yāsīn

excerpt: You are the best community brought forth for mankind. You enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong, and you believe in Allah.
source_location_hint: excellence of Ummah and virtue argument

excerpt: And appoint for me a helper from my family. (Prayer of Moses to Allah)
source_location_hint: Moses' request for Aaron as minister

excerpt: We have granted your prayer, O Moses.
And my brother Aaron—he is more eloquent than I. Send him with me as a helper, to confirm me. Verily I fear they will deny me.
We shall strengthen your arm through your brother, and give you both authority, so that they shall not be able to reach you. By Our signs, you and those who follow you shall be victorious.
source_location_hint: divine grant to Moses and Aaron

excerpt: These are they whom Allah has guided. So follow their guidance.
Nothing is said to you except what was said to the Messengers before you.
source_location_hint: parallel of Muhammad with previous prophets

excerpt: We have ordained for you the same religion which We enjoined upon Noah, and that which We have revealed to you, and that which We enjoined upon Abraham, Moses, and Jesus: establish the religion and make no divisions therein.
source_location_hint: Sunnah of Allah in prophetic succession

excerpt: This has been the way with the messengers We sent before you, and you will find no change in Our way.
That has been the way of Allah with those who passed away before, and you will find no change in the way of Allah.
ʿAlī's position in relation to me is like the position of Aaron in relation to Moses, except that no prophet will come after me.
source_work: Kitab al-ʿAlim wa-l-Ghulam or al-Majalis al-Muʾayyadiyya
source_author: Jaʿfar ibn Mansur al-Yaman or al-Muʾayyad fi'l-Din

excerpt: Verily, those who believe and do righteous deeds are the best of creatures.
source_location_hint: designation of believers as best creatures

excerpt: Allah chose Adam, Noah, the family of Abraham, and the family of ʿImrān above all the worlds.
And We saved the Children of Israel from the humiliating torment.
O Messenger, We have sent you as a witness upon Us
source_location_hint: sura al-Aḥzāb or similar context of prophethood

excerpt: I did not know the truth save through that which Allah taught me, and whatever I accomplished, Allah and ʿAlī accomplished it with me
source_author: Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him)
source_location_hint: transmitted by Ismaili da'wa sources and Shiite exegetes

excerpt: O Beloved, as you have testified to Our creation, unity, and lordship, so too have We sent two witnesses to your prophethood
source_location_hint: taʾwīl of Qur'anic verses on testimony

excerpt: When the hypocrites come to you, they say: 'We bear witness that you are the Messenger of Allah,' and Allah knows you are His Messenger—yet Allah bears witness that the hypocrites are liars
ʿAlī is with the Qur'ān, and the Qur'ān is with ʿAlī
source_work: Ḥadīth al-Thaqalayn transmission and Shiite hadith corpora
source_author: Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him)
source_location_hint: widely transmitted in Shiite and Ismaili sources

excerpt: There is no dry or moist thing but is recorded in the Clear Record (al-Kitāb al-Mubīn)
We have encompassed all things within the Manifest Imām (al-Imām al-Mubīn)
source_location_hint: Sura al-Yāsīn 36:12 (taʾwīl interpretation in Ismaili tradition)

excerpt: O ʿAlī, you are my brother in this world and in the Hereafter
source_work: Ḥadīth transmission in Shiite and Ismaili sources
source_author: Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him)
source_location_hint: widely attested in Ismaili and Twelver Shiite tradition

excerpt: Allah bears witness that there is no god but He, and so do the angels and the possessors of knowledge
I and ʿAlī are from one light, and you, O ʿAlī, are from one light
source_work: Ḥadīth transmission in Ismaili and Shiite tradition
source_author: Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him)
source_location_hint: transmitted in Ismaili da'wa and Shiite sources

excerpt: Knowledge without deed and deed without knowledge is misguidance
source_author: Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him)
source_location_hint: widely transmitted in Islamic scholarly sources
